Hey guys, welcome back. We are going to take a look right now at ThemePlay 9, which is the anti-launch launch. If you have access to the six-figure emails, ThemePlay Pack, then in session two, I believe you're going to have an even deeper dive into this one, a full hour long session, really detailing some extra nuances. But if you don't have access to that, this is what you're working with. Where you have everything you need in this mini lesson to really get this ThemePlay off the ground into your autoresponder and fired off on launch date to bring sales in right off the bat and launch in that powerful way. And what we're doing here with the anti-launch launch is this is the announcement. This is when you're saying to the world, my card is now open, my program is now available. Come and get it. But we're doing it in a way that's a pattern interrupt from the typical launch hype that makes it a big deal. Instead, you want to get your prospect face to face with the stakes and position the big deal not about you and your launch and how many years you put into it and how many, you know, how your pets miss you and all this stuff. But just the big deal is now how they can escape that pain. So keeping the focus on them. And I know it's hard. I know you put so much work into your launch and not only building your thing, but also building the marketing and executing it. And it's so hard to take that focus off us because we're proud of that and we should be proud of that. But there are other outlets to experience experience it and express it. And the launch day email isn't about you and all this hype and craziness around what went into it. It's about what your prospect can now overcome and escape and just getting them intimate with those stakes show them that room is still burning. So here is the container we're going to use to do it. You're going to lead with that link. You know, your offer is now live. They've seen enough in your pre-launch that, you know, they are ready to click through. Then we're going to offer some social proof or a core benefit of the product. We're going to bridge into why it's not an actual launch. And here's where it gets fun. So what is that Rome is burning moment? You know, what's burning? How have they been trying to extinguish it and describe that existing problem and context as specifically as you can? This is where you dial up the intensity of those flames and then remind them that that fire has burning long before you got there. It's been burning forever. So that's how you extend that consistent consistency principle into the indefinite past. And then you could get into why it's not a launch for you, you know, give credibility, proof and case studies, all the things you've kind of like been doing for months or years, you know, that went into this. So not just in the case of like building this program for years, but the insights and lessons and techniques have been in the field for years and performing. So that's where you extend your credibility into the indefinite past, if you will. Then you could call out a negative trait, kind of like procrastination and just hold them to that higher standard and then show them in your call to action the naturalness, the continuation of that small step. Remember, it's a lot easier to escape that fire if it's a small step on flat land than jumping out of a skyscraper. So make it as natural as possible. Remind them of the cost of waiting. You know, what will be left of Rome if you just continue to stand here? You know, those flames are just eating up more and more of it. Another call to action and then you can use the PS to disarm one or two key objections and really try to get that sale right off the bat, coach them right off the bat. We're going to address a key objection in spite of the rumors. Email marketing does not come down to dumb luck. It's not one size fits all. It's about learning and applying a proven repeatable set of email, copywriting techniques. That's the difference between emails that convert and emails that like induce unsubscribe whiplash. So just kind of like a little joke there and not to be confused with keyboard face where your dull, dull, dull email copy knocks your subscriber into a super slumber right at her desk. Now your emails don't have to be this long. This is more of a personality thing, you know, with Joanna and I, we do like to talk. We do like to write. So this could even be a little self indulgent at times, but you do have to have fun as a writer, especially if it's connecting and still entertaining your audience. Training program is also a total misnomer. So we could kind of overcome that second objection, you know, no one actually wants to invest time to learn copy for the sake of learning. They want to arm themselves with the skill to get a desired result. And that's true no matter what kind of educational program you're offering. The goal isn't to cram you full of concepts and jargon that make you look 5% smarter at your next marketing conference. If you want to look smart, just name drop Eugene Schwartz every chance you get done. The goal here is much more direct to arm you with the high action techniques that you can put into work and profit from immediately. So this is something we're using a lot of repetition around, you know, that this isn't this crazy long training. It's about getting results right away. Know what to write, why you're writing it and how to make a killer. No messing around. No going through a 50 hour Costco size video library. Most of our videos are about five minutes long actually. We're all too busy 10 X in. So just kind of outlining that speed of implementation, which is one of our big sales arguments for this previous launch we did back in July. Finally, PPPS. So this is how Rome will continue to burn until you give us to green light to come extinguish it for you. So that is the anti launch launch email. Stick with the structure and see how it plays out for you like all of these. It's not about length whatsoever. Length could be very much dependent on you, you as a writer, you as a communicator.
